input.background: Two trials looked at red wine consumption in lowering cholesterol levels in hypercholesterolemic men. In each trial, 25 men consumed 8 ounces of red wine for 14 days.
input.question: Is it reasonable to come to different conclusions for trial A and trial B?

output.answer: It is not reasonable to derive different conclusions because the observed mean changes are identical and the $P$-values are nearly identical, both providing tairh strong evidence against the null hypothesis.

trial.description: The identifier for the two trials conducted to study the effect of red wine consumption on cholesterol levels.

participants.description: The number of participants in each trial who consumed red wine.

red_wine_consumption.description: The amount of red wine consumed daily by participants during the trials, measured in ounces.

duration.description: The duration of the trials, measured in days.

cholesterol_levels.value: Unknown, to be measured in the trials
cholesterol_levels.description: The cholesterol levels of participants, which are the outcome variable being studied to determine the effect of red wine consumption.
